 {"id":"bd-807","title":"Implement do_pull()","description":"**Pull with strategy support**

## What
Function to pull updates with configurable strategy (ff-only, rebase, merge).

## Why
Different workflows need different pull strategies. ff-only is safe default, but some users prefer rebase.

## Implementation
```bash
do_pull() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local repo_name="$2"
    local strategy="${3:-ff-only}"
    local autostash="${4:-false}"

    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "[DRY RUN] Would pull: $repo_name (strategy: $strategy)"
        write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "dry_run" "" ""
        return 0
    fi

    local start_time
    start_time=$(date +%s)

    # Build pull args
    local pull_args=()
    case "$strategy" in
        ff-only) pull_args+=(--ff-only) ;;
        rebase)  pull_args+=(--rebase) ;;
        merge)   pull_args+=(--no-ff) ;;
    esac

    [[ "$autostash" == "true" ]] && pull_args+=(--autostash)

    # Get current HEAD for comparison
    local old_head
    old_head=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    local output
    if output=$(git -C "$repo_path" pull "${pull_args[@]}" 2>&1); then
        local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
        local new_head
        new_head=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        if [[ "$old_head" == "$new_head" ]]; then
            log_info "Already current: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "current" "$duration" ""
        else
            log_success "Pulled: $repo_name (${duration}s)"
            write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "updated" "$duration" ""
        fi
        return 0
    else
        local exit_code=$?
        local reason="failed"

        if [[ "$output" =~ (divergent|cannot\ be\ fast-forwarded) ]]; then
            reason="diverged"
            log_warn "Diverged: $repo_name (needs manual merge or --rebase)"
        elif [[ "$output" =~ (conflict|CONFLICT) ]]; then
            reason="conflict"
            log_error "Merge conflict: $repo_name"
        else
            log_error "Pull failed: $repo_name"
        fi

        write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "$reason" "" "$output"
        return $exit_code
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All strategies work
- Detects current vs updated (by comparing HEADs, not strings)
- Categorizes failures correctly
- Records results","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.455221388-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.455221388-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. All tests pass.","labels":["git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-807","depends_on_id":"bd-802","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.094464008-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-807","depends_on_id":"bd-405","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.126330962-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-808","title":"Create scripts/test_local_git.sh","description":"**Integration tests with local git repos**

## What
Test script that creates local git repos and tests operations against them.

## Why
We can't rely on network for CI tests. Local repos let us test all scenarios deterministically.

## Test Scenarios
```bash
# Setup
TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap "rm -rf $TEMP_DIR" EXIT

# Create 'remote' bare repo
git init --bare "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git"

# Clone and make commits
git clone "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git" "$TEMP_DIR/local"
cd "$TEMP_DIR/local"
echo "initial" > file.txt
git add . && git commit -m "Initial"
git push origin main

# Create 'projects dir' clone
git clone "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git" "$TEMP_DIR/projects/testrepo"

# Make new commit in 'remote' (via local)
echo "update" >> "$TEMP_DIR/local/file.txt"
git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" add . && git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" commit -m "Update"
git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" push

# Test: status should show 'behind'
# Test: pull should update
# Test: dirty detection
# Test: diverged detection
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run without network
- All status conditions tested
- CI can run this script","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:43:05.77151921-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:43:05.77151921-05:00","close_reason":"Created test_local_git.sh with 17 passing tests for git operations (is_git_repo, status detection, do_pull)","labels":["git","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-802","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.156263651-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.186539707-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.216416421-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-809","title":"Handle branch not found gracefully","description":"**Graceful handling when repo@branch branch doesn't exist**

## What
When user specifies repo@branch and branch doesn't exist, handle gracefully.

## Why
Branches get deleted, renamed, or typos happen. Current plan doesn't address this.

## Behavior
```
$ ru sync owner/repo@nonexistent
Cloning: owner/repo
  Warning: Branch 'nonexistent' not found
  Falling back to default branch (main)
```

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    # ... clone repo ...
    
    if [[ -n "$branch" ]]; then
        if git -C "$target_dir" rev-parse --verify "origin/$branch" &>/dev/null; then
            git -C "$target_dir" checkout "$branch" --quiet
        else
            log_warn "Branch '$branch' not found, using default branch"
            write_result "$repo_name" "clone" "branch_not_found" "" "Branch: $branch"
        fi
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clone succeeds with warning
- Default branch used as fallback
- Result recorded for summary
- User informed clearly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:59:41.810187043-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:59:41.810187043-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented graceful branch-not-found handling with warning and fallback to default branch","labels":["error-handling","git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-809","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.797824099-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-809","depends_on_id":"bd-902","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.828864235-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"bd-810","title":"Handle network timeouts gracefully","description":"**Handle network timeouts gracefully**

## What
Add timeout handling for clone and pull operations.

## Why
Flaky networks, slow connections, or overloaded servers can cause operations to hang indefinitely. Users need a way to bound wait time.

## Implementation
- Set GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT=1000 and GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME=30 as defaults
- These cause git to abort if transfer rate drops below 1KB/s for 30 seconds
- Add --timeout N option to override (sets GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME)
- On timeout, log error and continue to next repo
- Report timeouts distinctly in summary (not just 'failed')

## Acceptance Criteria
- Slow operations time out after reasonable period
- Timeout errors are clearly reported
- Other repos continue processing after timeout
- Timeout can be configured via flag or config","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T17:01:47.558978485-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T17:01:47.558978485-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented network timeout handling with GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_* env vars, --timeout flag, and timeout detection in clone/pull","labels":["resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.921445046-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.952318959-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.981841826-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ff8h","title":"Implement GraphQL batched repository discovery","description":"# Task: Implement GraphQL Batched Repository Discovery

## Purpose
Efficiently discover open issues and PRs across all configured repos using GraphQL alias batching, reducing API calls from O(n) to O(n/25).

## Background: Why GraphQL Batching?
- REST API: 1 call per repo for issues + 1 for PRs = 2n calls
- GraphQL aliases: Query 25 repos in single call = n/25 calls
- For 100 repos: 200 REST calls → 4 GraphQL calls (50× reduction)
- Avoids rate limiting, faster discovery

## Implementation Details

### gh_api_graphql_repo_batch()
```bash
gh_api_graphql_repo_batch() {
    local chunk="$1"
    local q="query {"
    local i=0

    while IFS= read -r repo_id; do
        [[ -z "$repo_id" ]] && continue
        local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
        local name="${repo_id#*/}"

        q+=" repo${i}: repository(owner:\"${owner}\", name:\"${name}\") {"
        q+=" nameWithOwner isArchived isFork updatedAt"
        # Issues with metadata for scoring
        q+=" issues(states:OPEN, first:50, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # PRs with metadata for scoring
        q+=" pullRequests(states:OPEN, first:20, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt isDraft"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # Oldest open issue for staleness
        q+=" oldestIssue: issues(states:OPEN, first:1, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:ASC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { createdAt }"
        q+=" }"
        q+=" }"
        ((i++))
    done <<< "$chunk"

    q+=" }"
    
    gh api graphql -f query="$q" 2>/dev/null
}
```

### parse_graphql_work_items()
Uses jq to flatten response into TSV for easy Bash parsing:
```bash
parse_graphql_work_items() {
    local resp="$1"

    echo "$resp" | jq -r '
        .data | to_entries[] | select(.value != null) |
        select(.value.isArchived != true) |
        select(.value.isFork != true) |
        .value as $repo |
        (
            # Issues
            ($repo.issues.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "issue", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, "false"] | @tsv
        ),
        (
            # PRs
            ($repo.pullRequests.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "pr", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, (.isDraft | tostring)] | @tsv
        )
    ' 2>/dev/null
}
```

### chunk_repo_ids()
Split repo list into chunks of 25:
```bash
chunk_repo_ids() {
    local chunk_size="$1"
    shift
    local repos=("$@")
    local count=0
    local chunk=""
    
    for repo in "${repos[@]}"; do
        chunk+="$repo"$'\n'
        ((count++))
        if [[ $count -ge $chunk_size ]]; then
            echo "$chunk"
            chunk=""
            count=0
        fi
    done
    
    [[ -n "$chunk" ]] && echo "$chunk"
}
```

### discover_work_items()
Main discovery function that:
1. Gets all repos from config
2. Resolves to owner/repo format
3. Filters non-GitHub hosts
4. Chunks into batches of 25
5. Executes GraphQL queries
6. Parses responses into work items
7. Calculates priority scores
8. Sorts by score descending
9. Applies max_repos limit if specified

## Data Format
Work item string format (pipe-separated):
```
repo_id|type|number|title|score|level|created_at|updated_at
owner/repo|issue|42|Authentication failing|85|HIGH|2024-12-15T...|2025-01-01T...
```

## Error Handling
- Skip repos that fail resolution
- Continue on individual query failures
- Log warnings for rate limit responses
- Graceful degradation if GraphQL unavailable

## Dependencies
- jq for JSON parsing (already assumed by ru)
- gh CLI with GraphQL support

## Testing
- Mock GraphQL response with fixture
- Verify chunking works correctly
- Verify archived/forked repos filtered
- Verify TSV parsing handles edge cases
- Verify 25-repo chunks respected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single API call for up to 25 repos
- [ ] Issues and PRs both discovered
- [ ] Labels extracted for scoring
- [ ] Archived/forked repos excluded
- [ ] Handles 100+ repos efficiently","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.462046988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:48:51.213603357-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:48:51.213603357-05:00","close_reason":"GraphQL batched discovery implemented: chunk_repo_ids(), gh_api_graphql_repo_batch(), parse_graphql_work_items(), repo_spec_to_github_id(), discover_work_items(). Batches up to 25 repos per API call. Tested: found 2 work items via --dry-run.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ff8h","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.851431049-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-fi65","title":"Implement basic mode TUI (gum-based fallback)","description":"Task: Implement Basic Mode TUI (gum-based)

Purpose
-------
Provide a simple but functional TUI when advanced dashboard not available,
using gum for styled output and selection.

When Used
---------
- gum available but not full TUI mode
- User prefers simpler interface
- Debugging/development

Implementation
--------------

show_question_basic_mode()
  Display single question with gum styling:
  
  gum style --border rounded --padding "1 2" \
    --border-foreground "#fab387" \
    "Question from: $repo"
  
  echo "$question"
  
  Build options array from JSON
  gum choose "${option_labels[@]}"

show_discovery_summary_basic()
  gum style --border rounded "Discovery Summary"
  echo "Repos with activity: $count"
  echo "Total work items: $items"
  echo "High priority: $high"

show_session_status_basic()
  Simple text status:
  for session in sessions:
    echo "$repo: $state"

basic_mode_loop()
  Sequential question processing:
  while questions pending:
    show_question_basic_mode
    answer = gum choose
    send_answer
    mark_answered

gum_confirm_wrapper()
  with fallback to read prompt:
  
  if GUM_AVAILABLE:
    gum confirm "$prompt"
  else:
    read -rp "$prompt [Y/n] " yn
    case "${yn,,}" in y|yes) return 0 ;; esac

Progress Display
----------------
gum spin --title "Processing..." -- command
gum style --foreground "green" "✓ Done"

Formatting Helpers
------------------
format_priority_badge()
  case priority:
    CRITICAL: gum style --foreground "red" "CRITICAL"
    HIGH: gum style --foreground "orange" "HIGH"
    NORMAL: gum style --foreground "yellow" "NORMAL"
    LOW: gum style --foreground "gray" "LOW"

ANSI Fallback
-------------
If gum not available, use ANSI codes:
- Bold: \033[1m
- Colors: \033[31m (red), etc.
- Box drawing: Unicode ─│┌┐└┘

Testing
-------
- Verify gum styling renders
- Verify choice selection works
- Verify ANSI fallback works
- Test without gum installed

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Questions display with styling
- [ ] Options selectable via gum choose
- [ ] Progress shown with gum spin
- [ ] Works without gum (ANSI fallback)
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fi65","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:13.195307516-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gptu","title":"Implement rate-limit governor (adaptive concurrency)","description":"Task: Implement Rate-Limit Governor

Purpose
-------
Dynamically adjust parallelism based on real rate limit data from GitHub
API and model responses, preventing throttling and cascading failures.

Background: Why Adaptive?
-------------------------
Static parallelism is dangerous:
- Too high: hit rate limits, get blocked
- Too low: waste time when limits are fine
- Limits vary by time, account tier, prior usage

Solution: query real limits, adjust dynamically.

Governor State
--------------
GOVERNOR_STATE associative array:
- github_remaining: remaining API calls
- github_reset: when limit resets (epoch)
- model_in_backoff: currently backing off?
- model_backoff_until: when backoff ends
- effective_parallelism: current allowed sessions
- circuit_breaker_open: emergency stop?

Implementation
--------------

start_rate_limit_governor()
  Run in background during review:
  while review lock held:
    update_github_rate_limit
    check_model_rate_limit
    adjust_parallelism
    sleep 30

update_github_rate_limit()
  Query: gh api rate_limit
  Extract: .resources.core.remaining and .reset
  If remaining < 500: log warning, reduce parallelism

check_model_rate_limit()
  Scan session logs for 429/rate limit patterns
  If multiple recent hits:
    Set model_in_backoff = true
    Set backoff_until = now + 60 seconds

adjust_parallelism()
  Start with target = REVIEW_PARALLEL (default 4)
  
  If github_remaining < 1000: target /= 2
  If model_in_backoff: target = 1
  
  Count recent errors in sliding window
  If errors > 5 in 5 minutes:
    Open circuit breaker
    target = 0
    Log emergency pause
  
  Set effective_parallelism = target

can_start_new_session()
  Return false if:
  - circuit_breaker_open
  - model_in_backoff
  - active_sessions >= effective_parallelism
  Otherwise true

Circuit Breaker
---------------
Pattern for handling cascading failures:
- CLOSED: normal operation
- OPEN: too many failures, pause all
- HALF-OPEN: after cooldown, try one (not implemented yet)

Metrics Tracked
---------------
- API calls per minute
- 429 responses per session
- Session error rate
- Effective vs requested parallelism

Integration Points
------------------
- Called before starting new session
- Called in session monitor loop
- Updates TUI status display
- Writes metrics for analytics

Testing
-------
- Mock gh api rate_limit responses
- Simulate 429 responses
- Verify parallelism adjusts
- Verify circuit breaker triggers

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Queries real GitHub rate limits
- [ ] Detects model rate limits from logs
- [ ] Adjusts parallelism dynamically
- [ ] Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures
- [ ] Metrics tracked for analysis","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:18:06.99800939-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:18:06.99800939-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented rate-limit governor with: GOVERNOR_STATE tracking, GitHub/model rate limit detection, circuit breaker pattern, adaptive parallelism. 13 unit tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gptu","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:17:00.313011296-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hmw8","title":"Implement command validation and blocking","description":"Security: Validate commands before execution in agent sessions.

Three categories:

SAFE_BASH_COMMANDS (always allowed):
  git, grep, find, ls, cat, make, npm, cargo, go, python, pytest,
  shellcheck, eslint, prettier, jq, sed, awk, sort, diff

APPROVAL_REQUIRED_COMMANDS (human confirmation):
  rm, mv, cp (file operations)
  curl, wget (network)
  docker, kubectl (containers)

BLOCKED_COMMANDS (never allowed):
  sudo, su, chmod +x, chown, eval, exec

Special gh handling:
  READ allowed: gh issue view, gh pr view, gh issue list
  BLOCKED in Plan mode: gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr merge

validate_agent_command() checks command against lists, returns:
  0 = allowed
  1 = blocked
  2 = needs approval

Called by pre-exec hook in agent sessions.

Acceptance: Blocked commands rejected, approval commands queued, gh mutations blocked in Plan mode.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:25.152161697-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:09.080859708-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:09.080859708-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented command validation with 19 passing tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hmw8","depends_on_id":"bd-9s7y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:17.523931567-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-mcvj","title":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing","description":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing

Overview
--------
Security safeguards, analytics collection, and comprehensive testing
to ensure the review system is safe, measurable, and reliable.

Components
----------

7.1 Security: Command Validation
   Prevent dangerous commands:
   - Blocked list: sudo, rm -rf, eval
   - gh mutation blocking in Plan mode
   - Approval required for risky ops
   - Pre-exec hook validation

7.2 Security: Secret Scanning
   Detect secrets before push:
   - gitleaks integration
   - Regex fallback patterns
   - Block push on detection
   - Human review option

7.3 Metrics Collection
   Track review analytics:
   - Reviews per period
   - Issues resolved
   - Questions asked/answered
   - Time per repo
   - Decision breakdown

7.4 Decision Tracking
   Learn from past decisions:
   - Record decision patterns
   - Suggest based on history
   - Per-repo preferences
   - JSONL format for analysis

7.5 Analytics Dashboard
   Visualize review effectiveness:
   - Overview stats
   - Decision breakdown chart
   - Top repos by activity
   - Efficiency trend

7.6 Unit Tests
   Test individual functions:
   - GraphQL batching
   - Priority scoring
   - JSON parsing
   - State management

7.7 Integration Tests
   Test full workflows:
   - Discovery to apply
   - Question handling
   - Error recovery
   - Resume functionality

7.8 Test Fixtures
   Mock data for testing:
   - Claude stream examples
   - GraphQL responses
   - Review plan examples
   - Error scenarios

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Security controls prevent dangerous ops
- Metrics collected accurately
- Analytics dashboard works
- Unit test coverage adequate
- Integration tests pass
- Fixtures enable reliable testing

Estimated Effort
----------------
~500 lines (security/metrics)
~800 lines (tests)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:00.837840538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:00.837840538-05:00"}
 {"id":"bd-mnu9","title":"Add cmd_review() command skeleton with argument parsing","description":"# Task: Add cmd_review() Command Skeleton

## Purpose
Create the entry point for all review functionality - the cmd_review() function that will orchestrate the entire review workflow.

## Implementation Details

### Location in ru script
Add after cmd_prune() (around line 3634), following existing command pattern.

### Function Structure
```bash
cmd_review() {
    local mode="auto"
    local parallel=4
    local dry_run="false"
    local resume="false"
    local apply="false"
    local push="false"
    local priority_threshold="all"
    local max_repos=""
    local max_runtime=""
    local max_questions=""

    # Parse arguments
    parse_review_args "$@"

    # Check prerequisites
    check_review_prerequisites || exit 3

    # Generate unique run ID
    REVIEW_RUN_ID="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"

    # Acquire global lock
    acquire_review_lock || { log_error "Another review is running"; exit 1; }

    # Auto-detect driver
    [[ "$mode" == "auto" ]] && mode=$(detect_review_driver)

    # Discovery phase
    log_step "Scanning repositories for open issues and PRs..."
    local -a work_items
    discover_work_items work_items "$priority_threshold" "$max_repos"

    if [[ ${#work_items[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "No work items need review"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # Show summary
    show_discovery_summary "${work_items[@]}"

    if [[ "$dry_run" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Dry run - exiting without starting sessions"
        release_review_lock
        return 0
    fi

    # ... orchestration phases follow
}
```

### Arguments to Support
| Argument | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| --plan | (default) | Generate plans only, no mutations |
| --apply | false | Execute approved plans |
| --mode=MODE | auto | Driver: auto, ntm, local |
| --parallel=N | 4 | Concurrent sessions |
| --repos=PATTERN | all | Filter repos by pattern |
| --skip-days=N | 7 | Skip recently reviewed |
| --priority=LEVEL | all | Min priority threshold |
| --dry-run | false | Discovery only |
| --resume | false | Resume interrupted |
| --push | false | Allow pushing (with --apply) |
| --max-repos=N | unlimited | Cost budget |
| --max-runtime=MIN | unlimited | Time budget |
| --max-questions=N | unlimited | Question budget |
| --json | false | JSON output |

### Main Dispatch Addition
Add to case statement in main():
```bash
review) cmd_review "$@" ;;
```

### Help Text Addition
Add to show_help():
```
  review       Review GitHub issues and PRs using Claude Code
```

## Testing
- Verify --help includes review command
- Verify unknown args produce error
- Verify prerequisites check runs
- Verify dry-run exits cleanly

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cmd_review() function exists and is callable
- [ ] All arguments parsed correctly
- [ ] --help shows review command
- [ ] --dry-run works (even with stub discovery)
- [ ] Lock acquisition/release works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:52.969404017-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:18:34.751746619-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:18:34.751746619-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented review cmd skeleton + arg routing"}
